sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: UP.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 52, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'DOWN': 'Moves block down by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 8)', 'LEFT': 'Moves block left by ~5 cols; works from rows 25-29 corridor opening and rows 45-49 corridor horizontal; blocked from rows 30-44 (VERIFIED steps 6-22, 48-49)', 'RIGHT': 'Moves block right by ~5 cols (VERIFIED step 2)', 'UP': 'Moves block up by 5 rows (VERIFIED step 4)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'blue_templates': 'Two blue L-shapes in black frames (top-center and bottom-left) - purpose unknown', 'dark_gray_corridor': 'L-shaped passage inside gray room: vertical segment at cols 29-33, horizontal at rows 40-49 cols 14-33', 'orange_blue_block': 'Movable object: 4x2 orange on top + 4x3 blue below (total 4x5), moves in 5-cell steps', 'white_cross': 'Static reference marker at rows 31-32, cols 21-22'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'Yellow bar + red squares - likely timer/progress'}
- Cross-level notes: Block moves in 5-cell increments. Corridor has vertical segment (cols 29-33) and horizontal segment (rows 40-49, cols 14-33). LEFT works from rows 25-29 (top opening) and rows 45-49 (horizontal segment).
Current level:
- World model: Level 1. Block at rows 45-49, cols 19-22 (bottom-left, below white cross). Have tried all positions in 4x4 grid (rows 25-49, cols 19-38). None complete level. Must reconsider what the goal is.
- Goal model: Unclear - tried many positions without completing level. Need new hypothesis.
- Recent findings: ['Block can reach rows 45-49 (new row position)', 'LEFT works from rows 45-49 (horizontal corridor segment)', 'Block at rows 45-49, cols 19-22: level not complete', 'All positions in 4x4 grid (rows 25-49, cols 19-38) tried: none complete level']
- Open questions: ['What position or condition completes the level?', 'Do the blue L-shape templates indicate something about the goal?', "Is the goal about matching the block's shape to a template?", "Could the goal involve the block's orientation or color?"]
- Plan: Continue moving LEFT through corridor until block reaches white cross area or level completes
- Strategy log: ['Abandoned corridor navigation from bottom/middle - LEFT blocked from rows 30-44. Found entry only at top opening rows 25-29.', 'Tried all positions in 4x4 grid without success. Need new hypothesis about goal.']
- Failed probes: ['LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 35-39: blocked (step 6)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 40-44: blocked (step 11)', 'LEFT from cols 34-38 at rows 30-34: blocked (step 16)', 'RIGHT,LEFT,LEFT sequence: no extra movement (step 18)', 'All positions in rows 25-44, cols 19-38 tried: level not complete', 'Block at rows 45-49, cols 24-27: level not complete', 'Block at rows 45-49, cols 19-22: level not complete (step 49)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 51 actions; a strong score needs about 22 or fewer. You are 2.3x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the block moved UP from rows 40-44 to rows 35-39, cols 19-22. The orange part of the block is now visible just below the white cross. The level is still not complete.
Let me check the block's exact position and try moving UP again to reach rows 30-34, which would put the block overlapping with the white cross area.
Actually, looking at the image, the block is at rows 35-39, cols 19-22. The white cross is at rows 31-32, cols 21-22. If I move UP again, the block would be at rows 30-34, cols 19-22, which would overlap with the white cross.
Let me try UP again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Block at rows 35-39, cols 19-22. Try UP to reach rows 30-34 (overlapping white cross)
action(['UP'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"DOWN": "Moves block down by 5 rows", "LEFT": "Moves block left by ~5 cols", "RIGHT": "Moves block right by ~5 cols", "UP": "Moves block up by 5 rows"}, "object_taxonomy": {"blue_templates": "Two blue L-shapes in black frames", "dark_gray_corridor": "L-shaped passage", "orange_blue_block": "Movable object (4x5), moves in 5-cell steps", "white_cross": "Static reference marker at rows 31-32, cols 21-22"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "Yellow bar + red squares - timer"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": "Block moves in 5-cell increments"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 1. Block at rows 35-39, cols 19-22 after UP from rows 40-44.", "goal_model": "Maybe block needs to overlap white cross at rows 30-34, cols 19-22", "recent_findings": ["Block can move UP through rows 45-49, 40-44, 35-39"], "open_questions": ["Can block reach rows 30-34, cols 19-22?"], "current_plan": "Try UP to reach rows 30-34, overlapping white cross", "strategy_log": ["Tried all 16 positions in 4x4 grid without success, now exploring rows 30-34"], "failed_probes": ["All positions in rows 25-49, cols 19-38 tried except rows 30-34, cols 19-22"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
